stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of noise level using a noise meter. The measurements have a mean of 113.1 dB and a standard deviation of 15.6 dB. However, calibration tests reveal that a noise meter systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.9% and 8.6%. Estimate the true noise level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 113.1 dB. After correcting for the 6.2% bias: ≈ 120.6 dB. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.9%-8.6%) and sampling variability: [111.5, 129.9]. Point estimate: 120.6 dB.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
